seo.title: Building AI Email Assistant with Agno + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai email assistant with Agno + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building an AI Email Assistant with Agno enables intelligent email processing, intent detection, draft generation, and approval workflows in a lightweight, model-agnostic framework. Guardrails are essential due to the high-risk nature of email handling, where failures can lead to PII leaks, inappropriate communications, or confidentiality breaches. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure safe, reliable agent behavior.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Email Assistant Architecture with Agno
architectureOverview.description:
The AI Email Assistant uses Agno's Agent class to create a multi-modal pipeline that processes incoming emails. It starts with intent detection via LLM calls, extracts attachments using vision models, generates draft responses, and routes to approval workflows. Memory is maintained across sessions for context-aware replies, with tools for email sending and recipient validation.

Key components include a RouterAgent for intent classification, WorkerAgents for drafting and attachment handling, and a SupervisorAgent for final approval. Structured outputs enforce email schemas, while multi-modal inputs handle images/PDFs in attachments. This modular design scales but introduces risks in memory recall and output validation.

Vex integration wraps critical agent.run() calls to verify PII filtering, tone, accuracy, and confidentiality before execution.

risks.name: PII Leakage in Drafts
risks.description: Agent includes sensitive data like SSNs or emails in generated responses
risks.mitigation: Vex PII entity extraction and redaction before sending

risks.name: Inappropriate Tone
risks.description: Sarcastic or aggressive language in professional email contexts
risks.mitigation: Tone analysis guardrail with professional sentiment scoring

risks.name: Invalid Recipients
risks.description: Agent suggests or sends to incorrect/unverified email addresses
risks.mitigation: Recipient domain validation and internal directory lookup

risks.name: Fabricated Context Recall
risks.description: Memory recalls non-existent prior conversations leading to errors
risks.mitigation: Session memory verification against email thread history

implementation.title: Core Email Processing Agent
implementation.description: Basic Agno agent setup for email intent detection and routing
implementation.code:
from agno.agent import Agent
from agno.models.openai import OpenAIChat

email_agent = Agent(
    model=OpenAIChat(id="gpt-4o"),
    name="EmailIntentAgent",
    instructions="Classify email intent: reply, forward, delete, archive. Extract key facts.",
    add_base_tools=False
)

def process_email(email_content: str):
    result = email_agent.run(email_content)
    return result

implementation.title: Draft Generation with Structured Output
implementation.description: Agent generates email drafts with Pydantic schema validation
implementation.code:
from agno.tools.pydantic import PydanticTool
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field

class EmailDraft(BaseModel):
    subject: str = Field(..., description="Professional subject line")
    body: str = Field(..., description="Polite, accurate response")
    recipients: list[str]

class EmailDraftTool(PydanticTool):
    name: str = "email_draft"
    description: str = "Generate professional email draft"
    args_schema = EmailDraft

 draft_agent = Agent(
    model=OpenAIChat(id="gpt-4o"),
    tools=[EmailDraftTool()],
    instructions="Always generate professional drafts with correct recipients"
 )

implementation.title: Attachment Processing with Vision
implementation.code:
from agno.agent import VisionAgent

attachment_agent = VisionAgent(
    model=OpenAIChat(id="gpt-4o"),
    name="AttachmentProcessor",
    instructions="""Extract text from attachments. Identify: invoices, signatures, 
    contracts, images. Summarize content and flag sensitive data."""
)

def process_attachment(file_path: str):
    with open(file_path, 'rb') as f:
        result = attachment_agent.run(f.read())
    return result
